sentences.text: This chapter describes the D standard extension for double-precision floating-point, which adds computational instructions compliant with the IEEE 754-2008 arithmetic standard's binary64 format and operations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2

sentences.text: The D extension depends on the F extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2

sentences.text: The D extension widens the 32 floating-point registers, `f0-f31`, to 64 bits (FLEN=64 in ).
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State

sentences.text: The `f` registers can now hold either 32-bit or 64-bit floating-point values as described below in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State

sentences.text: When multiple floating-point precisions are supported, then valid values of narrower n-bit types, n<FLEN, are represented in the lower n bits of an FLEN-bit NaN value, in a process termed NaN-boxing.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: The upper bits of a valid NaN-boxed value must be all 1s.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Valid NaN-boxed n-bit values therefore appear as negative quiet NaNs (qNaNs) when viewed as any wider m-bit value, n < m {le} FLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Any operation that writes a narrower result to an 'f' register must write all 1s to the uppermost FLEN-n bits to yield a legal NaN-boxedvalue.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Floating-point n-bit transfer operations move external values held in the IEEE 754-2008 formats into and out of the `f` registers, and comprise floating-point loads and stores (FLn/FSn) and floating-point move instructions (FMV.n.X/FMV.X.n). A narrower n-bit transfer, n<FLEN, into the `f` registers will create a valid NaN-boxed value. A narrower n-bit transfer out of the floating-point registers will transfer the lower n bits of the register ignoring the upper FLEN-n bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: Apart from transfer operations described in the previous paragraph, all other floating-point operations on narrower n-bit operations, n<FLEN, check if the input operands are correctly NaN-boxed, i.e., all upper FLEN-n bits are 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: If so, the n least-significant bits of the input are used as the input value, otherwise the input value is treated as an n-bit canonical NaN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > NaN Boxing of Narrower Values

sentences.text: The FLD instruction loads a double-precision floating-point value from memory into floating-point register rd. FSD stores a double-precision value from the floating-point registers to memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: FLD and FSD are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned and XLEN{ge}64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: FLD and FSD do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The double-precision floating-point computational instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands and produce double-precision results.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Computational Instructions

sentences.text: Floating-point-to-integer and integer-to-floating-point conversion instructions are encoded in the OP-FP major opcode space. FCVT.W.D or FCVT.L.D converts a double-precision floating-point number in floating-point register rs1 to a signed 32-bit or 64-bit integer, respectively, in integer register rd. FCVT.D.W or FCVT.D.L converts a 32-bit or 64-bit signed integer, respectively, in integer register rs1 into a double-precision floating-point number in floating-point register rd. FCVT.WU.D, FCVT.LU.D, FCVT.D.WU, and FCVT.D.LU variants convert to or from unsigned integer values.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: For RV64, FCVT.W[U].D sign-extends the 32-bit result. FCVT.L[U].D and FCVT.D.L[U] are RV64-only instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: The range of valid inputs for FCVT.int.D and the behavior for invalid inputs are the same as for FCVT.int.S.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: All floating-point to integer and integer to floating-point conversion instructions round according to the rm field.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: Note FCVT.D.W[U] always produces an exact result and is unaffected by rounding mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: The double-precision to single-precision and single-precision to double-precision conversion instructions, FCVT.S.D and FCVT.D.S, are encoded in the OP-FP major opcode space and both the source and destination are floating-point registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: The rs2 field encodes the datatype of the source, and the fmt field encodes the datatype of the destination. FCVT.S.D rounds according to the RM field; FCVT.D.S will never round.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.D, FSGNJN.D, and FSGNJX.D are defined analogously to the single-precision sign-injection instruction. //FSGNJ.D, FSGNJN.D, and FSGNJX.D
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: For XLEN{ge}64 only, instructions are provided to move bit patterns between the floating-point and integer registers. FMV.X.D moves the double-precision value in floating-point register rs1 to a representation in the IEEE 754-2008 encoding in integer register rd. FMV.D.X moves the double-precision value encoded in the IEEE 754-2008 encoding from the integer register rs1 to the floating-point register rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: FMV.X.D and FMV.D.X do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Conversion and Move Instructions

sentences.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Compare Instructions

sentences.text: The double-precision floating-point classify instruction, FCLASS.D, is defined analogously to its single-precision counterpart, but operates on double-precision operands.
sentences.section: Preamble > "D" Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.2 > D Register State > Double-Precision Floating-Point Classify Instruction
